---
title: "M0 ignition training"
execute:
  eval: false
---

M0 detects the transition from background activity to sustained seasonal
activity. Training selects decision thresholds using historical seasons while
preserving the information boundary at every issue week.

## Workflow

```{r}
library(PAGe)

historical <- load_flu_hist()
tuning <- tune_m0(historical)
m0 <- build_m0(historical, tuning = tuning)
```

The tuning objective should be computed from held-out seasons. Manual ignition
labels may define the target used for evaluation, but they must not be exposed
as features to the held-out detector. Report early and late detection errors
separately; a mean error alone can hide operationally different failure modes.

## Runtime checks

At deployment, retain the weekly detector snapshots rather than only the final
ignition week. Confirm that each snapshot contains no observations after its
issue week and that a manual override, when used, is explicit and auditable.
The plotting functions in the M0 reference section compare weekly detector
state with historical truth and help diagnose unstable threshold behavior.
